---
title: "Lab Updates"
subtitle: "Research Findings & News"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
---

## Latest Lab News

Keep up with research findings and updates from the lab related to Arkansas agriculture and crop weather impacts.

---

## 2025 Updates

### June 2025 — Soil Water Monitoring Initiative Launches
The Arkansas Weekly Weather & Soil Water Reports platform is now live. This automated system provides weekly benchmarked analysis of weather and soil conditions across 10+ research stations and ~4,600 grid cells covering Arkansas cropland.

**Key Features:**
- Automated soil water modeling (bucket model)
- Historical benchmarking (1995–2020)
- 7-10 day forecasts
- Station-specific crop development indices
- Open-access data downloads

**First Report:** June 23, 2025

---

## Research Projects Using This Data

### Soybean Climate Adaptation Study
*PI: Dr. [Name]*

Using historical weather data and soil water projections to evaluate how soybean cultivar maturity groups and planting dates interact under climate change scenarios (+2°C warming).

**Data Used:** Historical weather records, soil properties, current forecasts

---

### Wheat Winter Hardiness Under Changing Winters
*PI: Dr. [Name]*

Analyzing early-season soil and air temperature patterns to predict winter wheat survival and yield potential.

**Data Used:** Fall/winter temperature trends, soil water status at dormancy

---

## Publication Highlights

### Recent Papers

- **[Title]** — *Journal Name* (2024)
  Uses historical weather benchmarking to establish baseline conditions for climate impact assessments.

- **[Title]** — *Agricultural & Environmental Letters* (2024)
  Demonstrates synergistic effects of cultivar and planting date shifts under warming scenarios.

---

## Upcoming Events

### Weekly Seminar Series
**Thursdays, 2:00 PM** — Room TBD

Join us for weekly discussions on seasonal weather, forecast outlooks, and crop management implications.

### Summer Workshop: Soil Water Stress Management
**August 15, 2025** — [Register](mailto:eelli@uark.edu)

Learn to interpret soil water data and forecasts for irrigation scheduling and crop management decisions.

---

## Data Access for Researchers

### Using Weekly Report Data in Your Research

All data generated by this platform is available for download as CSV files. Citation format:

> University of Arkansas Division of Agriculture. 2025. Arkansas Weekly Weather & Soil Water Reports. [Week of June 23, 2025]. Accessed from https://your-lab.uark.edu/weather-reports/data-downloads

### Data for Modeling
Need long-term weather data for crop simulations? Access our 30-year historical database:
- Daily temperature (high, low, mean)
- Precipitation totals
- Solar radiation
- Growing Degree Days (GDD)
- Calculated evapotranspiration

[Request Data Access](mailto:eelli@uark.edu?subject=Data%20Access%20Request)

---

## Collaboration Opportunities

We're interested in partnerships with:
- **Agricultural Extension Services** — Integrating reports into farmer decision support tools
- **University Research Teams** — Using our data and modeling framework
- **Private Companies** — Developing commercial agricultural applications

[Inquire about Collaborations](mailto:eelli@uark.edu?subject=Collaboration%20Inquiry)

---

## Team

**Project Lead & Data Science:** Dr. Eduardo Ellis
**Research Collaborators:** [Team Members]
**Website & Technical Development:** [Contributors]

---

## Archive

### Previous Announcements

**May 2025** — Weather API and data pipeline finalized
**April 2025** — Historical data compilation complete
**March 2025** — Project approved by Division of Agriculture

---

## Staying Updated

Subscribe to lab news and new report releases:

- **Email Newsletter** (coming soon): [Subscribe](mailto:eelli@uark.edu?subject=Subscribe%20to%20Lab%20Updates)
- **Twitter:** [@your-lab-handle](https://twitter.com)
- **GitHub:** [Latest commits](https://github.com/your-lab/ar-weekly-weather-soil-reports)
- **RSS Feed:** [Subscribe to reports](../feed.xml)

---

## Questions?

Contact us at [eelli@uark.edu](mailto:eelli@uark.edu) or open an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/your-lab/ar-weekly-weather-soil-reports/issues).
